events.title: SoH Transits Drop 95% Amid Iran-US Tensions
events.description: Daily vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz have dropped to 0.2m GT, over 95% below normal levels, due to renewed tensions and attacks on vessels in the Middle East Gulf following the breakdown of the Iran-US interim peace deal.

events.title: Ennore Coal Terminal increases max draft
events.description: Effective July 17, 2026, the maximum permissible draft for vessels at Ennore Coal Terminal in India has been increased from 16.00 metres to 18.00 metres.

events.title: Vessel boarded by unauthorized personnel off Yemen
events.location: Gulf of Aden, near Al Mukalla
events.description: A ship was boarded by unauthorized personnel 65 nautical miles south of Al Mukalla, Yemen, in the Gulf of Aden. The UK Maritime Trade Operations warned vessels to transit with caution and report suspicious activity.

events.title: Russia strikes Mykolaiv port in Ukraine
events.description: Russian drones attacked the Ukrainian port of Mykolaiv on July 17, causing further disruption to regional shipping and port operations amid ongoing conflict.

events.title: MSC updates freight rates from South Asia to Europe
events.description: MSC has announced new Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates from India, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, and Bangladesh to Europe, effective 1 August 2026. The rates will apply until further notice, with a maximum validity until 31 August 2026.

events.title: Maersk updates AC1 and AC2 services between Asia and Latin America
events.description: Maersk has updated its AC1 and AC2 services connecting Far East Asia with the West Coast of Latin America as part of its Modular Network strategy to enhance regional connectivity. The changes aim to improve service reliability and efficiency for trade routes between Asia and Latin America.

events.title: Pirates seize small product tanker off Yemen
events.description: A small chemical tanker near Yemen was seized by up to seven armed pirates after issuing a distress call on July 17. The incident poses a risk to maritime security in the region.

events.title: IAG Cargo increases UK-India freight flights
events.description: IAG Cargo will launch a third daily freight service between London Heathrow and Delhi starting 19 September 2026, expanding its UK-India network to 70 weekly flights. The service is operated by British Airways using a Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner.

events.title: Houthis warned to disrupt Red Sea oil route
events.description: Iran has reportedly instructed Yemen's Houthi movement to be prepared to shut down the Red Sea oil route in response to potential U.S. strikes on Iranian infrastructure, posing a major risk to global shipping.

events.title: Middle East conflict impacts 2026 air cargo rates
events.description: The ongoing war in the Middle East is expected to drive air cargo shipping rates up by 15% by 2026, according to market intelligence firm Xeneta, due to disruptions in freight flows.

events.title: Tanker Seized by Suspected Pirates in Gulf of Aden
events.location: Gulf of Aden, off Yemen coast
events.description: Armed assailants are believed to have boarded the chemical tanker Asana off the southern coast of Yemen in the Gulf of Aden, taking control of the vessel.

events.title: Shipping Threats Expand Across Gulf Region
events.description: INTERTANKO warns that security threats to commercial shipping have expanded beyond the Strait of Hormuz into the broader Gulf region, following recent attacks on merchant vessels.

events.title: Russian drone strikes damage ships at Ukrainian port
events.description: Russian drone strikes damaged foreign-flagged ships at a Ukrainian Black Sea port, killing three people and intensifying pressure on Ukraine's trade routes.

events.title: Iran Demonstrates Capability to Disrupt Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran has shown it can disrupt the Strait of Hormuz using speedboats, missiles, drones, and mines, challenging U.S. naval dominance in the region. This highlights ongoing risks to global oil shipments through this critical maritime chokepoint.

events.title: US and Iran attack each other's infrastructure
events.description: The United States attacked bridges and an airport in Iran, while Iran responded by striking a power and desalination plant in Kuwait, escalating tensions and risking further disruption to regional infrastructure and supply chains.

events.title: US-Iran attacks disrupt Persian Gulf supplies
events.description: US strikes on Iran have intensified, threatening Persian Gulf supplies and causing European natural gas prices to rise to €55.7 per MWh, the highest in nearly four months.

events.title: Tanker attacks disrupt Gulf oil flows
events.description: Renewed attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz are disrupting oil exports, causing the physical oil market to tighten and pushing Brent prices back into the mid-$80s. This reverses an earlier recovery in Persian Gulf flows and introduces fresh upside risks to price forecasts.

events.title: Hormuz Strait shipping disruption impacts LNG flows
events.description: Conflict in the Middle East has disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a key route for liquefied natural gas (LNG), sending shockwaves through global gas markets.

events.title: Black Sea conflict drives feed millers to switch to corn
events.description: Tensions in the Black Sea have increased freight and CFR destination rates, prompting Asian feed millers to substitute wheat with corn in feed formulations due to rising wheat prices.

events.title: Black Sea shipping disruptions impact wheat prices
events.description: Vessel movement disruptions in the Kerch Strait and strikes in the Azov Sea have caused CIF Marmara wheat prices to rebound from a 19-month low, according to market participants.

events.title: QatarEnergy reduces LNG supply to Bangladesh due to Middle East war
events.description: Bangladesh's Petrobangla will receive only half of its planned 2026 LNG deliveries from QatarEnergy due to the war in the Middle East. This follows QatarEnergy's decision to halt LNG production in March.

events.title: New rail corridor boosts automotive logistics in Italy
events.description: Automar, part of the Grimaldi Group, has launched a new rail corridor connecting Livorno, Pontecagnano, and Gioia Tauro in partnership with FS Logistix and others, enhancing automotive logistics between central and southern Italy.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz security crisis impacts regional trade
events.description: A security crisis around the Strait of Hormuz is disrupting trade and prompting changes in port strategies across the Persian Gulf and Indian Ocean regions.

events.title: Sea Legend resumes Arctic route for China-Europe freight
events.description: Sea Legend Shipping is operating eight China-Europe sailings via the Northern Sea Route between August and October, with transit times of 21 days. This reflects a strategic shift in freight routing amid ongoing global supply chain dynamics.

events.title: EU lawmakers push to halt Arctic LNG tanker repairs
events.description: Over 100 European lawmakers have called on Danish shipyard Fayard to cease repairs on Arc7 ice-class tankers used for Russian Arctic LNG exports, targeting the last EU yard servicing the fleet.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Crisis Worsens with Containership Attack
events.description: A 7,000 TEU Global Feeder Shipping containership was attacked and caught fire in the Strait of Hormuz, forcing the crew to abandon ship. The incident has led to a sharp decline in traffic and renewed tensions between the US and Iran over the strait's status.

events.title: Iran urges Houthis to prepare to block Bab el-Mandeb Strait
events.description: Iran is reportedly urging Yemen's Houthi movement to prepare to shut down the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, a critical global shipping chokepoint, in response to potential U.S. attacks on Iranian infrastructure.

events.title: Iranian-linked tankers evade US blockade in Gulf of Oman
events.description: Two US-sanctioned Iranian-linked tankers are zig-zagging and making U-turns in the Gulf of Oman and Arabian Sea to avoid a US-enforced blockade. The vessels are part of a flotilla transporting Iranian energy exports amid heightened tensions.

events.title: EU Pressures Denmark to Stop Servicing Russian Arctic LNG Vessels
events.description: Over 100 European lawmakers have urged Denmark’s Fayard shipyard to cease servicing Russian Arctic LNG carriers, targeting the last European facility maintaining vessels for Moscow’s Yamal LNG project.

events.title: Yemen conflict escalates near Bab el Mandeb chokepoint
events.description: Political tensions in Yemen, exacerbated by extreme summer heat, are bringing the Bab el Mandeb Strait back to the brink of instability, raising concerns about potential disruptions to global shipping through this critical maritime chokepoint.

events.title: COSCO launches Peru-Costa Rica CHX3 service
events.description: COSCO SHIPPING Lines has launched a new weekly container service (CHX3) connecting Peru's Paita and Chancay with Costa Rica's Caldera, enhancing regional supply chain efficiency in Latin America.

events.title: U.S. Marines board falsified-flag tanker in Gulf of Oman
events.description: On July 16, the USS Boxer Amphibious Ready Group intercepted and boarded a 'shadow fleet' VLCC in the Gulf of Oman, part of a broader effort to counter illicit maritime activity.

events.title: Sea of Azov disruptions impact wheat supply
events.description: Renewed geopolitical tensions around the Sea of Azov are contributing to a surge in French wheat prices, as supply chain disruptions and weather risks in the EU further strain grain markets.

events.title: Port of Antwerp-Bruges cargo handling declines 2.4% in H1 2026
events.description: In the first half of 2026, the Port of Antwerp-Bruges handled 133.9 million tonnes of maritime cargo, a 2.4% decline compared to the same period in 2025. The decline is attributed to geopolitical tensions and trade conflicts affecting global freight flows.

events.title: Ukrainian port strikes disrupt grain trade
events.description: Grain traders in Ukraine have reduced local purchases due to intensified port strikes since July 10, with many halting operations as of July 15. Limited new purchases and existing port stocks are reported.

events.title: US-Iran hostilities escalate in Arabian Gulf
events.description: The US Central Command has launched strikes on Iranian sites along the southern coast, and disabled an Iran-linked oil tanker in the Arabian Gulf, escalating tensions between the US and Iran.

events.title: Shipping firms avoid Hormuz amid U.S.-Iran clashes
events.description: Global shipping companies are avoiding the Strait of Hormuz due to escalating U.S.-Iran tensions and a lack of trust in U.S. escort plans. The situation has increased risk for maritime transit through this key chokepoint.

events.title: Panamax congestion at Chinese ports
events.description: A surge in seaborne coal imports and typhoon-related delays have caused a growing queue of laden Panamax vessels waiting to berth in southern China.

events.title: Hormuz Strikes Disrupt Tanker Market
events.description: Recent strikes in the Middle East have caused the tanker market to return to a 'wartime pattern' of behavior, disrupting freight flows through the critical Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Hormuz tensions disrupt Persian Gulf crude flows
events.description: Renewed tensions at the Strait of Hormuz are causing tanker owners to consider shifting Persian Gulf crude flows to the US Gulf Coast. While activity remains limited, the situation is prompting concerns over potential disruptions to regional shipping.

events.title: Churchill Port resumes grain exports after six-year hiatus
events.description: Canada’s Port of Churchill is set to export grain for the first time in six years, signaling a revival of operations at the Arctic port. The move reflects efforts to expand and revitalize the facility’s role in the supply chain.

events.title: Ukraine reports 147 Russian shipping strikes
events.description: Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces struck 11 additional civilian vessels in Russian-controlled waters on July 16, raising the total to 147. The attacks disrupt shipping and heighten tensions in the Black Sea region.

events.title: GT Lines upgrades KIX 1 service to weekly frequency
events.description: GT Lines, part of Gulftainer, has upgraded its Khorfakkan India Express (KIX 1) service to a weekly schedule, enhancing connections between West India and the Gulf. The service began on 15 July 2026, calling at Nhava Sheva, Mundra, and Khorfakkan with vessels of 1,100-1,400 TEUs.

events.title: India Bans Seafarers from Hormuz Strait Transits
events.description: India has banned its seafarers from transiting the Strait of Hormuz following recent attacks on two ships on July 14, which resulted in one death and nine injuries.

events.title: Greece Blocks EU Sanctions on Yamal LNG
events.description: Greece has blocked the latest EU sanctions package on Russian energy exports, including Yamal LNG, to protect its domestic shipping interests. This move could disrupt EU efforts to isolate Russian energy and affect LNG freight flows.

events.title: US-Iran tensions threaten energy corridors
events.description: The United States launched fresh strikes against Iran, escalating tensions that could disrupt global energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for oil shipments.

events.title: Maersk adds low water surcharge on Europe-Canada trade
events.description: Maersk is introducing a Low Water Surcharge (LWC) on shipments from Europe to Montreal and Halifax, Canada, effective 1 August 2026. The surcharge applies to cargo moving to or via these ports, with rates set at US$150 per 20ft container and US$300 per 40ft container.

events.title: Ukraine strikes Russia-linked oil tankers in Black Sea
events.description: Ukrainian naval drones hit two large Russia-linked oil tankers in the Black Sea, expanding maritime conflict between Moscow and Kyiv and potentially disrupting regional shipping and energy flows.

events.title: Port of Antwerp-Bruges cargo volumes drop 2.4% in H1 2026
events.description: The Port of Antwerp-Bruges reported a 2.4% decline in cargo volumes for H1 2026, citing geopolitical tensions, trade conflicts, and operational disruptions as key factors affecting freight flows.

events.title: Maersk adjusts intermodal fuel fees in Europe
events.description: Maersk has updated its Intermodal Fuel Fees across multiple European markets due to ongoing volatility in global energy prices, particularly influenced by the security situation in the Middle East. The changes aim to maintain service continuity amid rising fuel costs.

events.title: ANL adjusts inland fuel surcharge in Australia
events.description: ANL has updated its Inland Emergency Fuel Surcharge in Australia due to ongoing global fuel market volatility. The carrier cited elevated diesel prices and increased inland transportation costs, which may impact supply chain operations.

events.title: US forces disable Iran-bound tanker in Arabian Gulf
events.description: U.S. forces disabled an unladen oil tanker in the Arabian Gulf that was attempting to sail toward Iran, violating a naval blockade. The incident could impact regional shipping and freight flows.

events.title: Ukraine drone strikes hit Russian-linked vessels in Black Sea
events.description: Ukraine reports striking 147 Russian-linked vessels over 11 days in a drone campaign targeting maritime logistics, expanding operations into the Black Sea.

events.title: Iran urges Houthis to block Red Sea oil route
events.description: Iran has instructed Yemen's Houthi movement to prepare to close the Red Sea oil route if the U.S. strikes Iranian power infrastructure, threatening global energy supplies.

events.title: Maersk closes Harlingen inland container depot
events.description: Maersk will close its HOV Harlingen inland container depot in the Netherlands by 3 August 2026 as part of a network optimization. The move aims to streamline inland logistics, with customers redirected to nearby depots in Leeuwarden.

events.title: US imposes 25% tariff on Brazilian imports
events.description: The Trump administration will impose a 25% tariff on certain Brazilian imports starting 22 July, impacting selected goods while exempting those that could disrupt US supply chains.

events.title: Intra-Asia freight rates decline as early peak season ends
events.description: Intra-Asia freight rates have declined, indicating the end of an early peak season, with congestion in Southeast Asian ports beginning to ease. The Shanghai Containerised Freight Index and Drewry's Intra-Asia Container Index both reported rate drops in early July.

events.title: Antwerp Port Quays Reopen After Toxic Gas Leak
events.description: Some quays in Antwerp’s Deurganckdok port area have reopened following a toxic gas leak that hospitalized 155 people. Quay 1718 and parts of Antwerp Gateway are now operational, while Quay 1741 remains closed.

events.title: Floods disrupt Chittagong Port operations
events.description: Heavy rain has caused flooding at Chittagong Port, Bangladesh's main maritime gateway, disrupting cargo movement and delaying vessel sailings. Exporters are demanding compensation for damaged goods and supply chain delays.

events.title: India restricts seafarers from Hormuz voyages
events.description: India has instructed shipowners and recruitment agencies not to deploy Indian seafarers on vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz due to renewed regional conflict, signaling potential disruption to this critical maritime chokepoint.

events.title: US strikes Iranian oil tanker near Hormuz
events.description: The US struck a sanctioned Iranian oil tanker near Iran's main export terminal, escalating tensions and causing a decline in shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Greek Veto Blocks EU Russia Sanctions Affecting Arctic LNG Shipping
events.description: Greece has blocked the EU's 21st sanctions package against Russia, seeking exemptions for its Arctic LNG shipping interests. The delay could impact restrictions on Russian energy exports and affect specialized LNG carriers.

events.title: Russian oil refining at 21-year low due to drone strikes
events.description: Russian oil refining volumes have dropped to a 21-year low at 3.80 million barrels per day due to damage from Ukrainian drone strikes, according to Kpler. The attacks have caused significant refinery downtime and disrupted energy production.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz disruptions impact petroleum markets
events.description: Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz during Q2 2026 caused volatility in crude oil prices and prompted international buyers to seek alternative supply sources for petroleum products.

events.title: US-Iran tensions escalate, impacting oil markets
events.description: Renewed military actions by the US against Iran and potential escalation are causing concern in the physical oil market, with oil prices rising due to fears of disrupted supply flows.

events.title: Black Sea grain corridor under threat from conflict
events.description: Escalating Ukrainian and Russian strikes in the Black Sea are raising concerns over disruptions to a key global grain export corridor. Ukrainian forces have targeted Russian vessels, impacting freight flows.

events.title: South Korea forms labor-management council for shipbuilding workforce gaps
events.description: The South Korean government launched a tripartite labor-management council to address workforce issues in the shipbuilding industry, including skilled worker shortages and job insecurity. The initiative involves the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Resources and the Ministry of Employment and Labor.

events.title: US imposes 25% tariffs on Brazilian imports
events.description: The United States has imposed 25% tariffs on most Brazilian imports following a trade investigation. The move, directed by President Donald Trump, is expected to disrupt trade flows and increase costs for Brazilian exporters.

events.title: China launches weekly Arctic container service
events.description: Sea Legend Shipping is launching a weekly container service via the Northern Sea Route from China to northern Europe, marking a shift in Arctic freight utilization.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Disruption Aftereffects
events.description: Christoph Toepfer warns that disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz may become a recurring issue, affecting global energy trade and tanker operations.

events.title: US military strikes tanker near Iran
events.description: The US military fired missiles at a Curaçao-flagged tanker, the Belma, as it approached Iran's Kharg Island. The vessel was disabled after ignoring warnings, raising concerns over regional tensions and potential disruptions to oil exports.

events.title: Port of Long Beach channel deepening approved
events.description: A key congressional committee approved a $255 million project to deepen the main shipping channel at the Port of Long Beach, aimed at accommodating larger vessels and improving cargo flows.

events.title: ARA Bunker Fuel Availability Tight
events.description: Bunker fuel availability is tight in the Amsterdam-Rotterdam-Antwerp (ARA) region for prompt supplies. Buyers are advised to book fuel supplies 5-7 days in advance to ensure good coverage from suppliers.

events.title: Iran threatens to halt energy exports amid U.S. strikes
events.description: The U.S. reimposed a naval blockade on Iran and intensified airstrikes, prompting Iran to threaten halting energy exports. The conflict risks disrupting a key global energy chokepoint.

events.title: US reimposes naval blockade on Iranian ports
events.description: The United States has reimposed a naval blockade on Iranian ports and launched fresh strikes, escalating tensions and potentially disrupting regional shipping and trade routes.

events.title: Suez Canal reopening could trigger port congestion
events.description: The potential reopening of the Suez Canal is expected to unleash additional shipping capacity, which could lead to fresh port congestion and impact container freight rates, particularly on Asia-Europe trade routes.

events.title: Ukraine intensifies Black Sea anti-shipping operations
events.description: Ukraine is expanding its anti-shipping campaign into the Black Sea, targeting Russia-linked merchant ships. The move could disrupt regional freight flows and increase risks for commercial vessels operating in the area.

events.title: U.S. Disables Iran-Linked Tanker
events.description: The U.S. military disabled an Iran-linked tanker with Hellfire missiles, marking the first enforcement action since reimposing a naval blockade on Iran's seaports. The incident could escalate tensions and disrupt regional shipping operations.

events.title: Toxic spill shuts down Antwerp container terminals
events.description: The Port of Antwerp-Bruges shut down several terminals due to a highly toxic acid spill from a shipping container, disrupting container operations.

events.title: Minerva orders four 6,000 TEU container ships
events.description: Greek shipping company Minerva Dry has ordered four 6,000 TEU container ships from Hengli Heavy Industries in Dalian, China, with expected delivery in 2028. The newbuildings signal a strategic shift toward larger container vessels, potentially impacting future freight capacity and market dynamics.

events.title: White House Considers Jones Act Waiver Extension
events.description: The White House is considering extending Jones Act waivers to allow foreign ships to transport goods between U.S. ports due to renewed conflict near the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns about energy prices and supply disruptions.

events.title: Supertankers Targeted in Hormuz Ship Attacks
events.description: Supertankers are increasingly being targeted in recent attacks near the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, amid the ongoing Iran war, raising concerns over supply chain security and energy transport.

events.title: Red Sea Shipping Route Under Threat from Iran-Houthi Tensions
events.description: Iran has warned it may expand its campaign to disrupt shipping from the Strait of Hormuz to the Red Sea, leveraging its Houthi allies in Yemen, in response to potential U.S. attacks.

events.title: Gibraltar-Spain border removal affects customs procedures
events.description: The Gibraltar-Spain border was removed on July 15, 2026, introducing new customs and cargo procedures. Crews will continue to transit through Gibraltar under revised protocols.

events.title: US removes Hormuz transit fee, shifts to Gulf investments
events.description: US President Donald Trump announced the removal of a 20% reimbursement fee for transits through the Strait of Hormuz, replacing it with trade and investment deals with Gulf states. The policy shift could impact shipping costs and regional economic dynamics.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd implements GRI for North America shipments
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd has announced a General Rate Increase (GRI) for shipments from the Indian Subcontinent, Pakistan, and the Middle East to the United States and Canada, effective for containers gated in from 15 June 2024.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz faces heightened geopolitical risk
events.description: War risk insurance premiums in the Strait of Hormuz have surged 33 times above normal due to escalating geopolitical tensions. Shipowners are grappling with uncertainty as misdeclared hazardous cargo and shifting priorities from efficiency to resilience complicate operations.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Tensions Disrupt Supply Chains
events.description: Rising geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz are causing disruptions to global freight and energy supply chains, compounded by surging diesel prices and increased energy market volatility.

events.title: Trump Policy Shift Impacts Strait of Hormuz
events.description: President Trump's administration is shifting from a proposed toll system to a full trade blockade against Iran, creating immediate pressure on global supply chains and causing port congestion and rising shipping rates.

events.title: Wildfire threatens CN train crew in Canada
events.description: A wildfire in British Columbia, Canada, has overtaken a Canadian National (CN) train, forcing the crew to plead for help. The incident highlights a significant disruption to rail operations in the region.

events.title: U.S. Strikes Iran's Coastal Defense Sites
events.description: The U.S. conducted strikes against Iran's coastal defense systems and cruise missile sites, escalating tensions that could impact regional energy chokepoints and shipping routes.

events.title: CMA CGM launches KORA EXPRESS service to West Africa
events.description: CMA CGM has launched the KORA EXPRESS service, offering a weekly container shipping route between Northern Europe and West Africa. The service aims to improve transit times, capacity, and reliability for key markets including Ghana, Nigeria, and Benin.

events.title: Ukrainian drone strikes disrupt Black Sea shipping routes
events.description: Ukrainian drone attacks have targeted Russian shadow fleet vessels in the Black Sea, disrupting freight flows and effectively closing a key maritime chokepoint without a formal blockade.

events.title: Adani completes fifth terminal at Mundra Port
events.description: Adani Ports is set to begin operations at its fifth container terminal (CT5) at Mundra Port in October, with an initial annual capacity of 1 million TEU, increasing port capacity amid growing competition along India's western coast.

events.title: Toxic gas leak closes Antwerp container terminals
events.description: A toxic gas leak from a container at the Port of Antwerp forced terminal closures and hospitalised over 150 people. The incident occurred at Deurganck dock on 14 July, causing significant disruption to port operations.

events.title: AGI accuses union of misleading campaign costing $20m
events.description: Alliance Ground International (AGI) claims the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) ran a misleading campaign that cost it nearly $20m in airline contracts. AGI has filed an injunction to stop the campaign.

events.title: US Threatens Escalated Action Against Iran Over Hormuz Strait
events.description: President Donald Trump pledged to increase US attacks on Iran until it stops targeting ships in the Strait of Hormuz and allows the waterway to remain open, escalating tensions that could disrupt global shipping.

events.title: China launches weekly Arctic container service to Europe
events.description: China’s Sealegend Shipping plans to launch the first scheduled weekly container shipping service between China and Europe via the Northern Sea Route this summer, signaling a shift in Arctic freight operations.

events.title: Iran conflict risks supply chain stability
events.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey highlighted risks to financial stability from renewed US-Iran hostilities, which could disrupt global supply chains, particularly through the critical Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Iran Threatens to Block Oil/Gas Export Routes
events.description: Iran has threatened to close all oil and gas export routes from the Gulf, escalating tensions with U.S. forces. This could disrupt a major global energy chokepoint and impact global supply chains.

events.title: MSC Erica calls at Karachi Port, 20,000-TEU capacity
events.description: Karachi Port handled the 20,000-TEU MSC Erica, one of the world's largest container ships, demonstrating its capability to accommodate ultra-large vessels. The call marks a milestone for Pakistan's container gateway.

events.title: DP World invests in Fujairah port as Hormuz remains closed
events.description: DP World is investing hundreds of millions to expand Fujairah port, signaling a long-term shift due to ongoing closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz closure disrupts shipping
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz has been closed to vessels following renewed tensions between Iran and the US, pushing fuel costs upward and disrupting global shipping flows.

events.title: Toxic chemical leak halts Antwerp-Bruges port operations
events.description: A toxic chemical leak aboard an MSC container ship at the Port of Antwerp-Bruges triggered an emergency response and temporarily halted shipping operations. The incident occurred at Deurganck Dock, part of Europe's second-largest port.

events.title: Norway offshore strike ends
events.description: Norway’s offshore oil service strike has ended after a wage dispute was referred to a voluntary wage board. The strike had disrupted offshore drilling and reduced petroleum output.

events.title: South Korea probes container manufacturers over price-fixing
events.description: South Korea has launched an antitrust investigation into four major container manufacturers, including China International Marine Containers (CIMC) and Singamas, following similar actions in the United States. The probe focuses on alleged price-fixing during the pandemic, which could impact global container supply and freight costs.

events.title: Rising containership fire incidents
events.description: Container ship fires have increased for three consecutive years, raising concerns about safety and potential disruptions to global freight flows.

events.title: US revives Iran blockade
events.description: The US has reimposed a blockade of Iranian ports, disrupting a key global shipping chokepoint. This follows a reversal of a planned toll on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Ukraine strikes Russian vessels in Sea of Azov
events.description: Ukraine has struck 116 Russian vessels in nine days in the Sea of Azov, marking an intense campaign that surpasses historical levels of merchant shipping attacks, such as the 1980s tanker wars.

events.title: Trump's Hormuz toll plan raises oil supply risks
events.description: President Donald Trump's proposal to impose a 20% fee on cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz has reignited concerns over potential disruptions to global oil supplies, especially if renewed conflict closes the critical shipping route.

events.title: Singapore VLSFO Fuel Availability Tightens
events.description: Very Low Sulfur Fuel Oil (VLSFO) availability in Singapore remains tight, with recommended lead times increasing to 15–18 days. Several suppliers are reporting low stock levels, indicating potential challenges for maritime fuel supply.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz vessel attacks raise LNG bunker prices
events.description: Attacks on merchant vessels in the Strait of Hormuz have raised concerns over LNG supply, leading to a surge in LNG bunker prices. Rotterdam's LNG price has increased significantly amid renewed US-Iran tensions.

events.title: Iran attacks US assets and shipping in Gulf
events.description: Iran launched attacks on US military facilities and commercial shipping in the Gulf, raising concerns over the stability of the Strait of Hormuz. The United Arab Emirates reported two Emirati oil tankers were struck.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic drops to 11 ships after US-Iran attacks
events.description: Only 11 ships passed through the Strait of Hormuz on July 12, the lowest since June 14, following attacks on the container ship GFS Galaxy and retaliatory strikes between the US and Iran. The reduced traffic highlights significant disruption to a key global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Disrupts Shipping
events.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz following US-Israeli military operations against Iran in late February 2026 caused significant disruptions across shipping markets, affecting asset values and ordering activity globally.

events.title: Middle East chemical trade disrupted by shipping bottlenecks
events.description: Shipping bottlenecks in the Middle East are impacting chemical trade flows, with uncertainty tied to the ongoing US-Iran conflict. A swift return to pre-conflict trade levels is considered unlikely.

events.title: Proponent selected for Roberts Bank Terminal 2
events.description: The Vancouver Fraser Port Authority has selected TerraMarine as the preferred proponent for the landmass and wharf component of the Roberts Bank Terminal 2 Project. This marks a key step in advancing a major infrastructure development at the port.

events.title: Decrease in Maritime Piracy and Robbery in Asia
events.description: The first six months of 2026 saw a strong decline in reported incidents of piracy and armed robbery against ships in Asia, indicating improved maritime security in the region.

events.title: Cameroon Port Reopens After Ship Collision
events.description: Operations at Cameroon’s Port of Douala-Bonabéri have resumed following a collision between a bulker and a cargo ship. The incident caused a temporary shutdown of the primary port, disrupting freight flows.

events.title: Aging Bulker Splits and Sinks Off Bandar Abbas
events.description: An aging bulk carrier split in half and partially sank off the coast of Bandar Abbas, Iran, an area with ongoing hostilities. The incident could impact regional shipping and raise concerns about vessel safety in the region.

events.title: Maersk cancels AC1 voyage on Far East-Latin America route
events.description: Maersk has removed one round voyage on its AC1 service connecting the Far East with the West Coast of Latin America. The cancellation affects voyage 632E departing Ningbo on 8 August 2026 and the corresponding westbound voyage 636W.

events.title: Maersk adds peak season surcharge on Turkey-Somalia trade
events.description: Maersk has introduced a Peak Season Surcharge (PSS) on container shipments from Turkey to Somalia, effective 28 July 2026. The surcharge is $200 per 20ft container, $300 per 40ft and 45ft containers.

events.title: U.S. Sanctions Iranian Shipping Network
events.description: The Trump administration imposed new sanctions on a major Iranian shipping and oil trading network, intensifying economic pressure and resuming a naval blockade, which could disrupt regional freight and trade flows.

events.title: US demands 20% Hormuz shipping fee amid conflict
events.description: Trump announced the US would reimpose a blockade and collect a 20% fee on all cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz, positioning the US as the 'Guardian of the Strait.' The move follows a major escalation with Iran, including US strikes on Iranian targets and retaliatory attacks by the IRGC.

events.title: UAE Condemns Iran's Attack on Tankers in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The UAE condemned Iran's attack on two tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, which killed one and injured eight. The US responded with fresh strikes, escalating tensions in a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Hormuz Strait traffic subdued amid US-Iran tensions
events.description: Vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz remain subdued as renewed hostilities emerge between the US and Iran following a breakdown in ceasefire talks. The situation raises concerns over potential disruptions to global shipping flows through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Ukraine strikes 11 Russian vessels in Sea of Azov
events.description: Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces reportedly hit 11 Russian merchant vessels and a border patrol ship in the Sea of Azov, escalating disruptions to maritime operations in the region.

events.title: Trump Announces Hormuz Protection Fee Shift
events.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that Gulf states will now fund the protection of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, reversing a previous policy. This shift could impact security and stability in a critical global chokepoint.

events.title: Hormuz Strait access concerns impact shipping
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz, previously open to all navigation, is now a source of significant concern due to ongoing regional conflict, potentially disrupting global freight flows through this critical maritime chokepoint.

events.title: US Tariff Refund Deadlines and SME Challenges
events.description: US Customs has accepted over 70% of applications for refunds on Trump-era tariffs, with $80bn paid to claimants. Shippers are urged to submit claims quickly as deadlines approach, while small and medium enterprises face challenges in navigating the process.

events.title: Seafarer killed in Hormuz Strait conflict escalation
events.description: An Indian seafarer was killed and at least 10 others injured following missile attacks near the Strait of Hormuz. Tensions continue to rise in the region despite Trump declaring the strait 'open to all ship traffic.'

events.title: Iran Targets Tanker Near Omani LNG Terminal
events.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) targeted a tanker near the Omani LNG terminal, escalating tensions in the region and posing a risk to maritime freight flows.

events.title: Fire on US Ready Reserve Vessel in Baltimore
events.description: A fire broke out aboard a U.S. military cargo ship docked in Baltimore Harbor. The Baltimore City Fire Department responded to the incident.

events.title: ZIM and MSC expand Asia-Jacksonville container service
events.description: ZIM and MSC have added Jacksonville Port (JAXPORT) to their ZCP-Amberjack Asia container service, enhancing weekly connections and expanding access to Latin America, the Caribbean, and the Mediterranean.

events.title: Maersk updates Egypt export booking rules under NAFEZA
events.description: Maersk has introduced new booking requirements for Egypt exports due to updated NAFEZA customs procedures, requiring a 19-digit Unique Consignment Reference (UCR) for compliance and cargo tracking, effective 17 July 2026.

events.title: Jaxport expands Asia-LatAm-Med container service
events.description: The Port of Jacksonville has been added to a new three-continent container service by two liner operators, enhancing connectivity between Asia, Latin America, and the Mediterranean.

events.title: Big Rig Crash Closes I-80 Near Donner Pass
events.description: A big rig crash closed eastbound Interstate 80 near Donner Pass early Tuesday, causing major traffic delays in California's Sierra Nevada region.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz and Russia impact diesel prices
events.description: Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, combined with developments involving Russia, are contributing to rising diesel prices after a period of declines.

events.title: Maersk resumes Red Sea route for West Africa service
events.description: Maersk has resumed its WAF6 service via the Red Sea, connecting the Middle East, Mediterranean, and West Africa. This marks a shift away from the Suez Canal route amid ongoing disruptions.

events.title: Southern Route of Strait of Hormuz No Longer Safe
events.description: The southern route through the Strait of Hormuz is no longer considered safe for transiting vessels, as shipowners avoid the Iranian side due to security concerns.

events.title: Ukraine reports 116 Russian ships hit in Sea of Azov
events.description: Ukraine claims to have damaged 116 Russian-linked vessels in the Sea of Azov over nine days, disrupting maritime operations in the region.

events.title: U.S. Abandons Hormuz Fee Proposal, Shifts to Gulf Investment Deals
events.description: President Trump has dropped his proposal to impose a 20% fee on cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz, opting instead to pursue trade and investment agreements with Gulf nations. The shift may impact freight flow policies and regional economic dynamics.

events.title: Iran signals renewed Red Sea shipping risk
events.description: Iran is signaling potential renewed pressure on Red Sea shipping beyond the Strait of Hormuz, following previous disruptions. This comes amid ongoing tensions involving Yemen's Houthi rebels.

events.title: Maersk increases ECS on India-Europe routes
events.description: Maersk has raised its Emergency Contingency Surcharge by $1,000 per container for shipments from the Indian Subcontinent to North Europe and the Mediterranean, effective 1 August 2026, impacting freight costs and operations.

events.title: Trump's Proposed 20% Cargo Fee on Hormuz Transits
events.description: A proposed 20% US cargo fee on shipments transiting the Strait of Hormuz could significantly impact global supply chains, influencing logistics pipelines, insurers, rail lines, and shipping flags.

events.title: Gioia Tauro Port Dredging Tender Launched
events.description: The Southern Tyrrhenian and Ionian Sea Port Authority has launched a tender for dredging works in the northern section of the Port of Gioia Tauro, near the Eranova dock and turning basin. The project is valued at EUR€2,628,355 and is part of broader port infrastructure improvements.

events.title: FedEx-CMA CGM Air Cargo Partnership
events.description: FedEx and CMA CGM plan commercial agreements for air cargo capacity, signaling a potential shift in air freight market strategies.

events.title: Typhoon Bavi disrupts North Asian ports
events.description: Typhoon Bavi caused significant disruptions in North Asian ports, with nearly 2m teu of containership capacity delayed. Congestion is concentrated in Shanghai and Ningbo, with 54% of global container port congestion now in North Asia.

events.title: Singapore Strait piracy crackdown reduces incidents
events.description: A coordinated crackdown in the Singapore Strait has significantly reduced reported incidents of piracy and armed robbery against ships in Asia, with 35 incidents recorded in the first half of the year—a 64% drop from the previous year.

events.title: Iranian Missiles Strike UAE Oil Tankers in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Two UAE oil tankers were struck by Iranian cruise missiles in the Strait of Hormuz, killing one Indian crew member and injuring eight others. The attack marks a significant escalation in the strategic waterway critical to global oil shipments.

events.title: Iranian Missiles Attack Tankers Near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Three more tankers were attacked near the Strait of Hormuz in escalating Iran-U.S. maritime conflict, with U.S. forces expanding strikes and the seafarer death toll rising.

events.title: Iran attacks Emirati tankers off Oman
events.description: Iran attacked two Emirati oil tankers near the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, killing one crew member and raising concerns over regional security and supply chain disruptions.

events.title: Tankers damaged in Strait of Hormuz attack
events.description: Two crude oil tankers operated by ADNOC Logistics and Services were struck by projectiles in the Strait of Hormuz on 14 July, resulting in one fatality and several injuries. The attack poses a significant risk to maritime freight flows through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Oman and UAE adjust Hormuz strategy
events.description: Oman and the UAE are shifting their approach to the Strait of Hormuz, opting for retained leverage over active management, signaling a strategic change in regional maritime influence.

events.title: ONE adjusts Scandinavia Express route to include Kalundborg
events.description: Ocean Network Express (ONE) is replacing Copenhagen with Kalundborg on its Scandinavia Express (SCX) service to improve connectivity in Denmark. The updated route will take effect with the Penang.

events.title: Russian drone strike kills 5 seafarers near Odesa
events.description: A Russian drone struck a Togo-flagged cargo ship discharging at a port in the Odesa region, killing five seafarers and injuring 12 others. The attack marks one of the deadliest incidents against commercial shipping since the start of the war.

events.title: Iran strikes UAE tankers in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: An Indian seafarer was killed and eight others injured when Iran struck two UAE-flagged tankers with cruise missiles in the southern Strait of Hormuz, within Omani territorial waters. The attack occurred as the US moved to reimpose its blockade of Iranian ports and charge for Hormuz transits.

events.title: Hormuz Strait tensions disrupt shipping
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz is experiencing heightened tensions with conflicting claims about its operational status, following a third round of US strikes and a drop in transits, creating uncertainty for global shipping.

events.title: DP World plans UAE port to bypass Strait of Hormuz
events.description: DP World is reportedly planning a new port and container terminal in the UAE to divert freight away from the Strait of Hormuz, likely in response to ongoing geopolitical tensions and potential disruptions in the region.
